@@ -550,6 +550,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-8tr","title":"ext4: Implement inode location math helper (inode -> table block + offset)","description":"Goal: given (inode_number, superblock fields, group desc), compute where the inode bytes live on disk.

Deliverables:
- Pure helper: inode_number -> (group, index_in_group).
- Use group desc inode_table pointer and inode_size to compute:
  - table_start_block
  - inode_byte_offset_from_table_start
  - absolute byte offset on device
- Handle ext4 inode numbering rules (inode numbers start at 1; 0 invalid).

Acceptance:
- Unit tests for boundary inodes: 1, inodes_per_group, inodes_per_group+1, last inode.
- Overflow/invalid inputs produce ParseError/FfsError (no panics).
- Helper is used by higher-level inode read code (ffs-inode later).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:17:15.392537885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:37:56.483745546Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:37:56.483718756Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8tr","depends_on_id":"bd-2w9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.687704093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-8vll5","title":"add btrfs FIEMAP edge-case coverage for inline and prealloc extents","description":"Survey finding: mounted-path btrfs FIEMAP coverage currently exercises only a plain regular file. In `ffs-core`, `btrfs_fiemap()` has special-case behavior for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), but those branches are not proven by harness coverage.

Scope:
- Add deterministic unit/conformance coverage for inline and prealloc btrfs extent items through `OpenFs::fiemap` or the closest stable lower-level seam.
- Where practical, extend mounted-path FUSE coverage to a real btrfs image that hits at least one non-plain extent shape, with soft-skip rules if the environment cannot create it deterministically.
- Assert logical offsets, physical offset conventions, and `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST` / `FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN` flag behavior.
- Update parity notes if the operator-visible claim needs qualification for these extent shapes.

Acceptance:
- Inline and prealloc btrfs FIEMAP branches are covered by deterministic tests.
- The test matrix makes it clear which btrfs extent shapes are proven only at unit/conformance level versus mounted path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:43.215379850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:10.385859555Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:10.383607060Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fiemap","fuse","ioctl","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-8wn1","title":"Add cargo-fuzz coverage for repair symbol parsers","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenKnoll","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:51:52.254705632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:59:20.873226560Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T23:59:20.869712020Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-8xbt0","title":"EXT4_IOC_PRECACHE_EXTENTS ioctl: extent metadata read-ahead hint","description":"EXT4_IOC_PRECACHE_EXTENTS = _IO(0x66, 38) = 0x6626 is a hint ioctl that asks ext4 to read all extent tree blocks for an inode into the page cache so subsequent reads do not stall on metadata I/O. e2fsprogs (e2fsck pass 1, debugfs dump_extents) and certain backup tools issue it before walking large fragmented files. Pre-fix dispatch_ioctl returns ENOTTY because the FUSE handler has no branch.

Implementation plan:
- ffs-core: add `FsOps::precache_extents(cx, scope, ino) -> Result<()>` trait method (default Ok(())).
- OpenFs override: ext4 reuses `collect_extents_with_scope` to walk the tree (no-op cache warm since FrankenFS keeps extent state in scope). btrfs returns Ok(()) (extent items are inline in btrfs, no separate tree to precache).
- ffs-fuse: const EXT4_IOC_PRECACHE_EXTENTS = 0x6626. dispatch_ioctl branch routes through `ops.precache_extents(...)`. No input/output payload.
- Tests: dispatch unit test (verifies routing through IoctlRecordingFs trace), short_input_returns_einval-equivalent if input is unexpectedly large, success returns IoctlResult::Data(empty).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:22:43.314497061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:15:45.222155668Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:15:45.220167164Z","close_reason":"EXT4_IOC_PRECACHE_EXTENTS dispatch + ext4 extent walk + btrfs inode read implemented; 2 dispatch regression tests; 285 ffs-fuse + 708 ffs-core green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-907l","title":"Apply worker_threads to FUSE mount queue settings","description":"MountOptions.worker_threads is currently informational only. Map explicit worker_threads values into mount options via max_background and congestion_threshold so it changes kernel-side request queue behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:24:30.145734334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:25:24.881117003Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:25:24.820870847Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","mount","performance"],"comments":[{"id":291,"issue_id":"bd-907l","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Mapped explicit MountOptions.worker_threads to kernel FUSE queue tuning options in build_mount_options: max_background=<threads> and congestion_threshold=<3/4 threads>. Added tests build_mount_options_includes_queue_tuning_when_worker_threads_explicit and build_mount_options_auto_worker_threads_omits_queue_tuning. Validation: cargo fmt; cargo fmt --check; targeted new tests; cargo check/clippy -p ffs-fuse --all-targets; cargo test -p ffs-fuse (full crate) all pass.","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:25:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-94hd","title":"Reject truncated btrfs send stream payloads","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:04:27.838115324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T04:09:39.423108671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T04:09:39.422847556Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-95zg","title":"Fix clippy or_fun_call in ffs-core btrfs context path","description":"rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_codex_frankenfs cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings fails in crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs at the btrfs context lookup because ok_or eagerly constructs FfsError::Format. Scope is to switch that site to ok_or_else, re-run remote clippy, and keep the fix tightly bounded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-08T19:08:56.813572210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T19:09:56.516840942Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T19:09:56.516568537Z","close_reason":"Replaced ok_or with ok_or_else in crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs and validated with rch cargo clippy plus rch cargo fmt --check","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
